title: Four definitions to change the world: Struggles over meaning in the UN tax convention negotiations

title: ITAT Excludes Incomparable Companies as Functional Differences & Turnover Distorted TP Analysis

title: ITAT Deletes Section 56(2)(viib) Addition as AO Changed Valuation Method Contrary to Rule 11UA

title: Bengaluru ITAT: Apartment Owners’ Welfare Association Not Taxable at Maximum Marginal Rate

title: Section 50CA Valuation Rejected as Rule 11UA Ignored Unrealizable Assets: ITAT Chennai
summary: ITAT Chennai held that unrealizable assets of a company under insolvency could not be included while computing fair market value under Rule 11UA. ai_summary: The ITAT Chennai rejected a Section 50CA valuation that ignored unrealizable assets of a company under insolvency, ruling that Rule 11UA should reflect the fair market value. The tribunal directed recomputation using the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) auction price, rather than the tax officer's initial artificial valuation.

title: TP Adjustment Set Aside for Failure to Prove Profit Shifting Under Section 80-IA(10)
summary: The ITAT held that a transfer pricing adjustment under Section 80-IA(10) cannot be sustained without proving the statutory conditions, including close connection, arranged business transactions, and more than ordinary profits. The Tribunal deleted the adjustment for lack of foundational evidence. The post TP Adjustment Set Aside for Failure to Prove Profit Shifting Under Section 80-IA(10) appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that a transfer pricing adjustment under India's Section 80-IA(10) cannot be sustained without explicit proof of profit shifting, including close connection and transactions yielding more than ordinary profits. The Tribunal deleted the adjustment due to the tax authorities' failure to establish the foundational statutory conditions.

title: ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as Earlier Coordinate Bench Ruling Was Binding
summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment of ₹61.22 crore after finding the facts identical to an earlier assessment year. It held that a pending High Court appeal does not dilute the binding nature of an unreversed Tribunal decision. The post ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as Earlier Coordinate Bench Ruling Was Binding appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment of ₹61.22 crore, asserting that a previous coordinate bench ruling with identical facts for an earlier assessment year was binding. The Tribunal emphasized that a pending High Court appeal does not diminish the precedential value of an unreversed Tribunal decision.

title: Transfer Pricing Addition Deleted Because ALP Was Fixed at Nil Without Prescribed Method
summary: ITAT ruled that determining the arm's length price of management service fees at Nil without following a prescribed transfer pricing methodology was not sustainable. The Tribunal upheld the CIT(A)'s decision to delete the transfer pricing additions related to both the management fee and the manufacturing segment.

title: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as TPO Used Ad Hoc Method Instead of Section 92C
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that transfer pricing adjustments for intra-group services were unsustainable because the TPO determined the arms length price without applying any prescribed method under Section 92C. title: TPO Cannot Value Management Fees at Nil Without Rejecting Evidence of Services Rendered
summary: The ITAT Delhi held that the Transfer Pricing Officer could not arbitrarily determine the arm's length price of management services at nil despite evidence establishing receipt of services. The Tribunal reinstated the assessee's TNMM-based benchmarking, reinforcing the need for substantiated transfer pricing adjustments in India.

title: SC Remands Over 1000 Reassessment Appeals After Finance Act 2026 Changed Law
summary: The Supreme Court set aside High Court judgments quashing reassessment notices after noting that the Finance Act, 2026 introduced clarificatory amendments affecting the competence of Assessing Officers. title: Cross-Border ESOPs in India: Unresolved Taxation Gap Silently Costing Mobile Professionals
summary: The article highlights that Indian tax law lacks a statutory formula for allocating cross-border ESOP income, increasing the risk of double taxation. The post Cross-Border ESOPs in India: Unresolved Taxation Gap Silently Costing Mobile Professionals appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as TNMM Was Consistently Accepted in Earlier Years
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) remained the most appropriate method as there was no change in the assessee's functional profile and earlier Tribunal decisions had consistently accepted it. The transfer pricing adjustment based on the internal Cost Plus Method was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as TNMM Was Consistently Accepted in Earlier Years appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) remained the most appropriate method for the assessee. The Tribunal emphasized that there was no change in the assessee's functional profile and that earlier decisions had consistently accepted TNMM, overriding an adjustment based on the internal Cost Plus Method.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 69C Addition as Source of Expenditure Was Fully Explained
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that Section 69C cannot be invoked where expenditure is duly recorded in the books and its source is fully explained. It deleted the addition relating to royalty, commission, and technical service payments. The post ITAT Deletes Section 69C Addition as Source of Expenditure Was Fully Explained appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that Section 69C, pertaining to unexplained expenditure, cannot be invoked where expenditure is duly recorded in the books and its source is fully explained. The Tribunal consequently deleted additions related to royalty, commission, and technical service payments, affirming that these expenditures were adequately substantiated.

title: ITAT Quashes Assessment as Final Order Was Passed Beyond Section 144C(13) Time Limit
summary: Tribunal held that final assessment order was time-barred because it was passed after mandatory period prescribed under Section 144C(13). title: ITAT Quashes ₹85 Crore TP Adjustment as TPO Order Lacked Digital Signature
summary: The ITAT held that a transfer pricing order issued without authentication or a digital signature is invalid in law. title: ITAT v Adopts CBDT-Approved APA Rate for Royalty and FTS ALP
summary: The ITAT held that the royalty and FTS adjustment was excessive and directed the Assessing Officer to apply the 1.9% rate accepted by the CBDT under the Unilateral Advance Pricing Agreement. title: Section 263 Revision Invalid as AO Had Already Examined Key Issues: ITAT Visakhapatnam

ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a Section 263 revision is invalid if the Assessing Officer had already conducted inquiries and applied due diligence to the disputed issues. This decision limits the Commissioner's power to revise orders where prior examination has occurred.

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as Valid Return Was Not Filed Under Section 139

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as No Valid Return Was Filed: ITAT Visakhapatnam
